title: Dino Egg Discovery in the Desert
story: Imagine finding the first-ever dinosaur eggs! That's exactly what happened to American explorer Roy Chapman Andrews in 1923. While searching the vast Gobi Desert in Mongolia on July 13, Andrews stumbled upon an incredible treasure. These weren't just any old rocks - they were fossilized dinosaur eggs! This groundbreaking discovery helped scientists learn more about how dinosaurs reproduced and cared for their young.

follow_up_question: Why do you think finding dinosaur eggs was so important for scientists?

title: America's First Numbered Patent
story: Choo-choo! In 1836, the United States Patent Office rolled out a big change. After issuing nearly ten thousand patents without numbers, they decided to start fresh. It was for an improvement in train wheels, helping locomotives run more smoothly on the tracks.

bonus_fact: Before this, the US had already issued about 9,957 unnumbered patents!
